query: Jharkhand Rajya Sabha election June 2026 BJP candidate nomination contested
answer:
## Jharkhand Rajya Sabha Election June 2026 — BJP Candidate Nomination & Contest

### Overview

The Election Commission of India announced on **May 22, 2026** that biennial elections for **24 Rajya Sabha seats** across 10 states (plus 2 bypolls) will be held on **June 18, 2026**. **Two seats from Jharkhand** are up for election.

---

### Seats at Stake

| Seat | Incumbent | Party | Status |
|------|-----------|-------|--------|
| Seat 1 | Deepak Prakash | BJP | Term expiring June 21, 2026 |
| Seat 2 | Shibu Soren (deceased) | JMM | Vacant since Aug 4, 2025 (Soren died of chronic kidney disease & stroke, age 81) |

---

### Election Timeline

| Event | Date |
|-------|------|
| Notification | June 1, 2026 |
| Last date for filing nominations | June 8, 2026 |
| Scrutiny of nominations | June 9, 2026 |
| Last date for withdrawal | June 11, 2026 |
| Poll & Result | **June 18, 2026** |

---

### BJP Candidate Nomination Status (as of June 3, 2026)

- **BJP has NOT yet officially announced a specific candidate** for the Jharkhand seat.
- BJP has **signalled intent to contest** despite lacking the numbers. Two senior BJP leaders have confirmed this publicly:
  - **Babulal Marandi** — BJP Leader of Opposition in the Jharkhand Assembly
  - **Aditya Sahu** — BJP State President (also a sitting RS member from Jharkhand)
- The nomination deadline is **June 8, 2026**, so an announcement is imminent.

---

### Electoral Math — Why This Is Contested

The election uses **Single Transferable Vote (STV)** with proportional representation:

**Winning quota = (81 MLAs / 3) + 1 = 28 votes per seat**

| Alliance | MLAs | Quota Coverage |
|----------|------|----------------|
| **Ruling MGB/INDIA Alliance** | **56** | Covers 2 full quotas (2 × 28 = 56) |
| JMM | 34 | — |
| Congress (INC) | 16 | — |
| RJD | 4 | — |
| CPI(ML)(L) | 2 | — |
| **Opposition NDA** | **24** | **4 votes short** of even 1 quota |
| BJP | 21 | — |
| AJSU | 1 | — |
| JD(U) | 1 | — |
| LJP(RV) | 1 | — |
| **Others (JLKM)** | **1** | — |

**Key finding:** The ruling alliance can comfortably win **both seats** if party discipline holds. BJP/NDA's 24 MLAs fall **4 votes short** of the 28 needed for one seat. BJP would need **cross-voting from at least 4 ruling alliance MLAs** to win.

---

### Contest & Controversy

- **JMM horse-trading complaint (May 25, 2026):** JMM formally wrote to the Election Commission alleging BJP is attempting to influence ruling alliance MLAs through "financial inducements, unethical external pressure, and intimidation." JMM urged CBI, ED, DRI, CVC, and the Jharkhand Anti-Corruption Bureau to remain vigilant.

- **BJP's historical "extra candidate" strategy:** BJP has a documented pattern of filing nominations even without assured numbers, relying on opposition cross-voting. From 2020–2026, this strategy was attempted **9 times** with a **67% success rate** (6/9 wins), succeeding in Gujarat 2020, Maharashtra 2022, Haryana 2022, Himachal Pradesh 2024, UP 2024, and Odisha 2026.

- **Anti-defection law does NOT apply** to Rajya Sabha elections, meaning cross-voting carries only internal party consequences (suspension/expulsion), not disqualification from the legislature.

---

### Alliance Seat-Sharing (Ruling Side)

- **Congress** has expressed interest in one of the two seats. Congress spokesman Rakesh Sinha stated: "Congress aims to contest from one of the seats. A talk will be held with the partners."
- Expected arrangement: **one seat for JMM, one seat for Congress**.

---

### Current Jharkhand RS Composition & Projected Shift

| Member | Party | Term End | Status |
|--------|-------|----------|--------|
| Deepak Prakash | BJP | Jun 2026 | Retiring |
| Vacant (Shibu Soren) | JMM | Jun 2026 | Vacant |
| Aditya Sahu | BJP | Jul 2028 | Continuing |
| Mahua Maji | JMM | Jul 2028 | Continuing |
| Pradip Kumar Varma | BJP | May 2030 | Continuing |
| Sarfaraz Ahmad | JMM | May 2030 | Continuing |

**Current:** BJP 3, JMM 2, 1 vacant → **Projected if alliance discipline holds:** BJP 2, JMM-INDIA alliance 4.

---

### Status

As of **June 3, 2026**, the election has **not yet taken place**. Nominations are still open (deadline June 8). The contest hinges on whether BJP files a candidate and whether cross-voting materializes.

key_facts: Two Jharkhand Rajya Sabha seats are up for election on June 18, 2026: one retiring BJP incumbent (Deepak Prakash) and one vacant since JMM's Shibu Soren died on August 4, 2025.
BJP has NOT officially announced a candidate as of June 3, 2026, but has signalled intent to contest; nomination deadline is June 8, 2026.
The winning quota is 28 votes per seat (81 MLAs in Jharkhand Assembly).
Ruling MGB/INDIA alliance holds 56 MLAs (JMM 34, Congress 16, RJD 4, CPI-ML-L 2) — exactly enough for 2 full quotas.
Opposition NDA holds only 24 MLAs (BJP 21, AJSU 1, JD-U 1, LJP-RV 1) — 4 votes short of one quota.
BJP would need cross-voting from at least 4 ruling alliance MLAs to win a seat.
JMM wrote to the Election Commission on May 25, 2026 alleging BJP horse-trading attempts with ruling alliance MLAs.
BJP's historical 'extra candidate' strategy (filing despite lacking numbers) has a 67% success rate (6/9) from 2020-2026.
The anti-defection law (10th Schedule) does NOT apply to Rajya Sabha elections, so cross-voting does not lead to disqualification.
Congress has expressed interest in contesting one of the two Jharkhand seats; expected seat-sharing is one JMM, one Congress.
Current Jharkhand RS composition is BJP 3, JMM 2, 1 vacant; if alliance discipline holds, it would shift to BJP 2, JMM-INDIA 4.
BJP State President Aditya Sahu and Leader of Opposition Babulal Marandi have both publicly confirmed BJP's intent to field a candidate.
